title: Cintas (CTAS) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Cintas (CTAS) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 9.0% growth rate recognizes Cintas's exceptional execution in a fragmented market. It reflects a combination of steady organic volume growth, aggressive cross-selling of higher-margin ancillary services, and structural margin improvements derived from increasing route density.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.0% discount rate is utilized. The business model is highly resilient and cash-generative, yet its reliance on general business employment levels introduces moderate cyclical sensitivity that must be accounted for.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 3.0% serves as the terminal growth rate. As a largely domestic, industrially focused company, Cintas's ultimate long-term growth ceiling is inextricably linked to U.S. GDP growth and inflation.

thesisParagraphs: Cintas represents one of the highest-quality compounders in the industrial sector. Its moat—built on route density, scale economies, and long-term customer lock-in—is extraordinarily wide. The company has essentially perfected the model of acquiring smaller local operators, stripping out redundant administrative costs, and plugging their routes into its hyper-efficient national infrastructure.

However, a great business is not always a great investment at any price. The market clearly recognizes Cintas's quality and has awarded it a premium valuation multiple that leaves very little room for error. While its cash flow will likely continue to compound reliably, the current price assumes near-flawless execution and uninterrupted economic expansion. My analysis suggests a significant margin of safety is currently absent.
verdictFooter: Based on trailing financials. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 9.0% growth rate for Cintas?
faq.answer: Gemini selected a 9.0% growth rate based on Cintas's historical ability to generate high single-digit top-line growth while structurally expanding operating margins through route density efficiencies.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for Cintas's DCF?
faq.answer: An 8.0% discount rate balances the company's exceptional, stable cash generation against its underlying sensitivity to macroeconomic employment cycles.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict regulatory actions, geopolitical shifts, or black swan economic events.
